page: Amazon Web Services c4.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2666 v3 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 2.9 GHz. The processor includes 25 MB of L3 cache and supports instruction sets such as AVX, AES, BMI2, and FMA. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 1.88 GB per core, and operates on the Xen hypervisor. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate moderate single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling. The single-core stress-ng score of 1480.59 and Geekbench single-core score of 1149.0 fall into the average tier, supported by steady single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression rates. However, multi-core benchmarks show constraints, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 2516.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 3903.16, both categorized as weak. Memory performance is also a bottleneck, with a PassMark memory score of 1088.78 and uncached read/write bandwidths hovering around 25.92 GB/sec and 22.39 GB/sec respectively.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4.xlarge offers a dedicated compute profile for legacy workloads without the overhead of modern high-density instances, though its resource density is lower than newer generations. Given the lack of local storage and modest memory bandwidth, this instance is best suited for compute-bound tasks with low memory footprints, such as basic web serving, single-threaded batch processing, and lightweight utility scripting. It is less optimal for memory-intensive databases or high-throughput multi-threaded applications.
description: Amazon Web Services c4.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of memory, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2666 v3 processor running at 2.9 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. Operating under the Xen hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and bundled GPUs, relying on a 1 Gbps baseline network connection. Performance benchmarks reveal average single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1149.0, but show weaknesses in multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth, as evidenced by a PassMark memory score of 1088.78. Qualitatively, the c4.xlarge provides a dedicated, budget-friendly compute option for legacy workloads. It is best suited for lightweight compute tasks, single-threaded batch processing, and basic web serving, while being less effective for memory-intensive databases or modern multi-threaded applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c4.xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and a 1 Gbps network baseline, suited for lightweight single-threaded batch processing.

tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance featuring Intel Xeon processors and balanced single-core performance for lightweight batch processing workloads.

Xen hypervisor architecture with 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average single-core performance suitable for single-threaded batch tasks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Best suited for lightweight web serving and basic compute-bound workloads
